agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement the Order Fulfillment state management system. Bird has defined the domain invariants governing order state transitions and refund eligibility. MJ has designed the architecture: a PATCH endpoint for state changes, an OrderStateMachine for transition validation, and a separate RefundService that verifies order state before issuing refunds. Your job is to implement all three components with the domain rules baked in.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"An order cannot transition from SHIPPED to PENDING","testable_assertion":"OrderStateMachine.transition(SHIPPED, PENDING) throws/returns ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"rule":"A refund can only be issued on a DELIVERED order","testable_assertion":"RefundService.issue(orderId) where order.state != DELIVERED throws/returns REFUND_NOT_ELIGIBLE"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"State transitions validated in an OrderStateMachine class","implementation_note":"OrderStateMachine owns the full transition matrix. The PATCH endpoint delegates to it — no transition logic in the controller. This keeps the invariant testable in isolation."},{"decision":"PATCH /orders/{id}/state endpoint for state changes","implementation_note":"Accepts a target state in the request body. Returns the updated order on success, or a structured error (including error code) on invalid transitions."},{"decision":"RefundService is separate and calls the order service to verify state","implementation_note":"RefundService must query current order state before issuing. Do NOT embed refund eligibility logic inside OrderStateMachine — keep the bounded contexts clean. RefundService depends on the order service, not the other way around."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Invalid state transition is rejected","given":"An order in SHIPPED state","when":"A revert-to-PENDING transition is attempted via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The request is rejected with error code ORDER_STATE_INVALID"},{"criterion":"Refund on non-DELIVERED order is rejected","given":"An order in any state other than DELIVERED","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The request is rejected with an error indicating refund is not eligible"},{"criterion":"Refund on DELIVERED order succeeds","given":"An order in DELIVERED state","when":"A refund is requested via RefundService","then":"The refund is issued successfully"},{"criterion":"Valid state transitions succeed","given":"An order in a state with a valid forward transition (e.g., PENDING to SHIPPED)","when":"The transition is requested via PATCH /orders/{id}/state","then":"The order state is updated and the updated order is returned"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"Order state invariant","technical_term":"Transition validation rule in OrderStateMachine","definition":"A domain constraint that forbids certain state transitions. Enforced by the state machine's transition matrix, surfaced as error codes at the API layer."},{"domain_term":"Refund eligibility","technical_term":"Pre-condition check in RefundService","definition":"The domain rule that refunds require DELIVERED state. Implemented as a guard clause in RefundService that queries order state before proceeding."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":[]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are clearly defined and non-contradictory","Architecture cleanly maps to domain rules — no ambiguity in ownership","Acceptance criteria directly derivable from Bird's invariants"],"low_confidence_areas":["Full set of valid state transitions not enumerated by Bird (only the SHIPPED->PENDING prohibition is explicit). Shaq may need to ask Bird for the complete transition matrix.","Error response shape not specified by MJ beyond error codes — Shaq has discretion on response body structure."],"assumptions":["The OrderStateMachine will be a whitelist model (only explicitly allowed transitions succeed) rather than a blacklist model (everything allowed unless blocked). This is safer and aligns with Bird's invariant-first framing.","RefundService communicates with the order service via direct in-process call, not over HTTP, unless MJ specifies otherwise."]}}
